title: Gooey Argentinian Yogurt Dumplings
description: Fermented, gooey dumplings made with lowfat Greek yogurt, whole wheat flour, and crushed green cardamom pods for a fragrant, indulgent treat.
instructions: In a bowl whisk together the whole wheat flour, baking powder, ground cardamom, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl mix the low‑fat Greek yogurt, honey, lemon zest, and egg white until smooth.
Add the wet mixture to the dry ingredients and stir until a soft dough forms; add water a tablespoon at a time if the dough feels too dry.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1.5 inches in diameter, and gently press each into a flattened disc.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and lightly oil it; place the dough discs in the pan and cook 3‑4 minutes on each side, until golden brown and puffed.
Serve the dumplings warm, drizzled with a little honey or a squeeze of fresh lemon for extra brightness.
Enjoy the creamy, fermented‑like texture that melts in your mouth.
